name: Single · $90k salary, $300k super, retire 67
notes: Transcribed from Moneysmart's Retirement Planner with assumptions aligned: investment return 6% (pre- and post-retirement), admin+investment fee 0.85%, fixed admin $74/yr, insurance $0; CPI 2.5%, salary growth 3.7% and pre-retirement deflator 3.7% (= CPI + 1.2% living standards), retirement deflator CPI 2.5%. Single homeowner, $0 outside super for the cleanest comparison. Super-at-retirement matches within ~1.7% (nominal accumulation agrees to <2%); money lasts the full horizon in both. The first-year Age Pension carries a WIDE 25% tolerance on purpose: our engine (~$18.4k) is assets-test bound on the current-FY single max ($31,223 incl. supplements), whereas Moneysmart's $14,364 implies an older max of ~$27,840 — a reference-data VINTAGE difference, not an engine discrepancy (our figure is the more current one). Revisit the tolerance when Moneysmart refreshes its Age Pension rates.
